---
title: "Overview of compound events"
---

::: {.callout-note}
Materials for this session will be posted shortly before the course.
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal" icon=false}
**Speaker**

[Chris White](../../about.qmd#chris-white) | University of Strathclyde · [🌐](https://www.strath.ac.uk/staff/whitechristopherdr/){target="_blank"}
:::

## Overview

This presentation introduces the multi-hazard and compound events dimension of
complex risk, and complements the broader multi-risk overview that follows. It covers three areas:

- **Compound event concepts and typologies**, including preconditioned, multivariate, temporally compounding, and spatially compounding events, and how these categories describe combinations of hazards and drivers that co-occur or cascade in space and time.

- **Methodological challenges** specific to each typology, and the statistical and copula-based approaches used for compound event analysis *[placeholder: confirm additional approaches covered]*.

- **Recent examples** of compound events that illustrate these concepts and
  challenges.

## Slides

*Slides will be posted shortly before the course.*
